events.description:
Saturdays, May – August: 7:30-9:30 am

Learn to observe and identify birds by sound and sight as you walk through the Garden and Wirth Park with a Garden Naturalist.

No registration necessary, meet at the Garden’s parking lot.

events.title: Rock the Block
events.description: The fun is all about out on the block this summer. Get outdoors and join us on the back patio and field as we bring out the music, dance, play some games, have a talent show, stay cool by the pool, and enjoy summertime in the city. It’s an active, fun adventure everyday and the staff at the park will make sure that it Rocks the Block! *No Program June 19th or July 4th.

events.title: Garden Storytime
events.description:
Thursdays, May 7-October 8, 10-10:30 am

Sundays, May 17-October 11, 10 am – 10:30am

Come for a nature-packed Storytime in the heart of the Wildflower Garden led by a Naturalist. Read stories and sing songs before exploring the trails or making a themed craft in the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter. Perfect for ages 0-7 with their caregivers present.

No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Tree Treks
events.description:
First Saturday of the month, 4-5 pm

June 6, July 4, August 1, 2026

Third Sunday of the Month, 2-3 pm

June 21, July 19, August 16, 2026

Come join a garden naturalist for a guided walk and learn how to identify common native trees by their leaves, bark, and fruits.

No registration necessary, meet outside the Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Go Skateboarding Day at Elliot
events.description: Join us for Go Skateboarding Day at Elliot Skate Park! There will be fun for the whole family with games, prizes, food trucks, music, and Skateboarding related activities! Show off your skills in a contest or learn new ones from our skate instructors. See demonstrations from the pros. Explore local vendors then skate off into the group ride through the city!

events.title: Afternoon Birding Stroll
events.description:
Tuesdays, June 2-30, 4-5 pm

Join a Naturalist for a late afternoon stroll on the trails in search of the various bird species that call the Garden home. Binoculars are available to borrow. Learn to identify birds by their plumage and songs while walking among the flowers.

No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Juneteenth Celebration: Movie “Harriet”
events.description: Come join Logan Park for an event honoring Juneteenth! We will be watching “Harriet”, Harriet (2019) dramatizes Harriet Tubman’s daring escape from slavery and her transformation into a fearless conductor on the Underground Railroad. We will have a discussion afterwards. We will also have Juneteenth trivia to learn more about the history of Juneteenth Holiday!

events.title: Downtown Parks & Netflix Present: KPop Demon Hunters Sing-Along at The Commons
events.description:
Grab your lightsticks and join Minneapolis Downtown Parks and Netflix for an exclusive outdoor screening of the official KPop Demon Hunters Sing-Along at The Commons Park in Downtown Minneapolis.

Celebrate one year of the award-winning film with fellow fans during a night filled with music, dancing, karaoke, local vendors, and a sunset sing-along under the Downtown skyline.

Before the movie begins, jump into Random Dance Play with MNKpopConnect, learn choreography inspired by the film, take the mic during KPop Demon Hunters-themed Karaoke, shop a K-pop Market featuring 10 local vendors, and connect with fellow fans.

Whether you’re a longtime fan or discovering the movie for the first time, this is a chance to celebrate the music, characters, and community that made KPop Demon Hunters a worldwide hit. Ready to save the world through the power of K-pop? This night is for you.

About the Screening

“Experience the global phenomenon like never before! Celebrate the One Year Anniversary of the award-winning film KPop Demon Hunters with an interactive outdoor screening of the official Sing-a-long version. We’re encouraging all fans to come in costume and sing along to the entire soundtrack. It’s a high-energy musical celebration for the whole family!”

Schedule

  • 6–8:30 pm | K-pop Market featuring 10 local vendors
  • 6–8 pm | Random Dance Play with MNKpopConnect
  • 6:45–7:15 pm | Learn the Moves from the Movie
  • 6–8:30 pm | KPop Demon Hunters-themed Karaoke
  • Sunset | KPop Demon Hunters Sing-Along Screening

Wednesday, June 24, 2026
The Commons Park
425 Portland Avenue South
Minneapolis, MN 55415

The celebration continues later this summer with the third annual K-pop in the Sunset event returning to The Commons on August 8, 2026.

Free admission. Costumes encouraged. Fan chants welcome.

Grab your lightsticks, gather your crew, and let us know you’re coming.

events.title: Meadow Makers
events.description:
Wednesdays, 9:30–11 am, May 20 – September 2, 2026

Join in the meadow-making in Theodore Wirth Regional Park!

Stop by the meadow off the entrance drive to the Eloise Butler Wildflower Garden and join in the weeding and planting fun.

No registration required. Stay for as little or as long as you’d like!

events.title: Invertebrate Discovery Walks
events.description:
Wednesdays, June 3 – June 24, 4:00pm – 5:00pm

Join a naturalist on a walk to discover the hidden kingdoms of insects and other invertebrates in the Garden! We’ll learn about invertebrate identification, their behavior, and ecology as we observe them on the trails.

No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Folwell Park Ribbon Cutting Celebration
events.description:
Wednesday, June 24 from 5 to 6 pm
1615 North Dowling Avenue

Bring friends, family and neighbors – all ages are welcome!

  • Skate the park—loaner boards available
  • Get creative with skateboard-based art activities
  • Enjoy light refreshments
  • Join the community ribbon cutting following remarks from Park Board leaders, including Charles Rucker, District 2 Commissioner (North Minneapolis)

events.title: Flower Hour
events.description:
Thursdays, May – August, 5-6 pm

Join a Naturalist for an hour-long evening walk through the Garden to see the seasonal wildflowers. The plants are the highlight here in this tour. Come learn and unwind in nature in the beautiful Garden.

No registration necessary, meet at the Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Knitting in Nature
events.description:
2nd and 4th Thursdays of the month, May – August from 6:00pm – 7:30pm

Bring your projects and join fellow crafters in the garden for knitting, crocheting, and crafting in community as Autumn settles in the Garden.

All experience levels are welcome!
Some chairs provided. No registration necessary, meet at the Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Cuentos Infantiles en el Jardín – Spanish Storytime
events.description:
Viernes, 5 de junio – 21 de Agosto, 10:00am – 10:30am

¡Cuentos y actividades sobre la naturaleza dirigidos por un naturalista!

No necesita inscribirse; nos reunimos fuera del edificio, Martha Crone Visitor Shelter. Niños de 0 a 7 años acompañados con un adulto.

_____________________________________________________________

Fridays, June 5 – August 21, from 10:00am – 10:30am

Stories and activities about nature led by a naturalist!

No registration is required; we meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

Ages 0-7 with caregivers present.

events.title: Ribbon Cutting at Lynnhurst Park
events.description:
The centerpiece climbing boulder will soon be installed at Lynnhurst Park’s newly improved playground. Once it’s in place, the Minneapolis Park and Recreation Board (MPRB) invites everyone to a ribbon cutting celebration!

Friday, June 26, 3:30 – 4:30 pm
Lynnhurst Park Playground
1345 W Minnehaha Parkway at West 50th Street

  • Meet your Park Board commissioner and Park Board and Lynnhurst Neighborhood Association leaders
  • Learn how Lynnhurst Park’s playground improvements are part of a 20-year initiative to fix up all Minneapolis neighborhood parks
  • Join in the community ribbon cutting photo with popsicles to follow!

Bring friends, family and neighbors – all are welcome.

events.title: Bike Help Days
events.description: Receive free bike maintenance and tips from professionals!

events.title: Making Space for Native Plants in Greater Eloise Stewardship Area
events.description:
Join the Greater Eloise Butler Stewards to remove invasive plants!

Dates: Second and fourth Sundays, 2-4:30pm

Location: Eloise Butler. Follow our signs from the Garden’s front gate kiosk to the weeding site.
We work adjacent to Eloise Butler Wildflower Garden in Theodore Wirth Park. (The Garden itself is maintained by dedicated Minneapolis Park Board staff. Our work helps protect their efforts.)

Supplies: We provide tools, bags, training and gloves. Bring your own water and snacks.

NOTE: There may be ticks and poison ivy in the area. Dress appropriately (long sleeves, pants, and closed-toe shoes).

No need to register ahead of time. No experience necessary.

Learn more here. link to https://www.friendsofeloisebutler.org/

events.title: Drop-in Series: National Hydration Day
events.description: National Hydration Day – Refresh & Recharge! Celebrate National Hydration Day with a refreshing drop-in program focused on the importance of staying hydrated and healthy! Stop by to create your own custom water bottle label, try fruit-infused water samples, and learn fun facts about hydration. Enjoy simple wellness-themed crafts. All ages welcome. No registration needed – just drop in and drink up!

events.title: Drop-in Series: National Patch Day
events.description: National Patch Day – Design Your Own Patch! Celebrate National Patch Day by creating a patch that’s uniquely you! Drop in to design your own fabric patch using felt, markers, fabric paint, and more. Whether you want to show off your interests, make a statement, or create something fun for your backpack or jacket, this is your chance to get crafty. All materials are provided – no sewing required! Perfect for all ages. No registration needed – drop in anytime during program hours and get creative!
